title: Dealer strategies
description: Practice understanding of dealer strategies when car buying

assessment_items.question_content:
You find a great-priced car advertised online. When you get to the dealership, they say it's sold but steer you towards a more expensive model.

**What deceptive strategy is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: hidden fees
bait and switch
financing trick
unnecessary add-on

assessment_items.question_content:
You're about to finance a car at the dealership and the salesperson offers you a loan with low monthly payments that seem too good to be true. Later, you realize the loan term is much longer than you discussed, causing you to pay more in interest.

**What deceptive strategy is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: financing tricks
extended warranties
bait and switch
pressure tactics

assessment_items.question_content:
You're finalizing the purchase of a new car and the dealer starts insisting on extra features like rustproofing and paint protection, claiming they're essential for your car's longevity and safety.

**What deceptive strategy is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: financing tricks
unnecessary add-ons
bait and switch
pressure tactics

assessment_items.question_content:
A dealer adds a security system to your car and includes it on your invoice, but you never requested this add-on. 

**What is the appropriate response?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: agree to the charge to avoid conflict
ask the dealer to remove the security system and the charge from the invoice
accept the security system but ask for a discount on the car
leave without purchasing the car

assessment_items.question_content:
You respond to an ad for a car at a great price, but when you arrive at the dealership, you're told that car is no longer available and are shown a more expensive model instead.

**What is your best course of action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: buy the more expensive model since you're already there
ask if there are any cars available within your original budget
accept the dealer's explanation and request to be notified if a similar car becomes available
leave and report the dealership for potentially deceptive advertising practices

assessment_items.question_content:
You're finalizing the financing for a car when the dealer offers you a surprisingly low monthly payment. Upon closer inspection, you realize the loan term is several years longer than you intended, significantly increasing the total interest you'll pay. 

**What is your best course of action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: accept the longer-term loan for the lower monthly payments
request a breakdown of the total interest paid over the loan term versus shorter-term options
agree to the terms now and plan to refinance the loan later
negotiate for a loan term that aligns with your original intention, even if it means higher monthly payments

assessment_items.question_content:
Reviewing car buying paperwork line-by-line allows you to spot any hidden fees or add-ons that you didn't agree to.

**Which of these is most likely a hidden fee?**

Description | Amount
-|-:
Car price | $\$9{,}500.00$
Sales tax ($8\%$) | $\$760.00$
Registration fee | $\$180.00$
Title transfer fee | $\$85.00$
Maintenance fee | $\$550.00$
**Total** | **$\$11{,}075.00$**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: registration fee
title transfer fee
maintenance fee

assessment_items.question_content:
Reviewing car buying paperwork line-by-line allows you to spot any hidden fees or add-ons that you didn't agree to.

**Which of these is most likely a hidden fee?**

Description | Amount
-|-:
Car price | $\$8{,}000.00$
Sales tax ($8\%$) | $\$480.00$
Registration fee | $\$180.00$
Documentation fee | $\$550.00$
Title transfer fee | $\$85.00$
**Total** | **$\$9{,}295.00$**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: documentation fee
title transfer fee
sales tax

assessment_items.question_content:
Reviewing car buying paperwork line-by-line allows you to spot any hidden fees or add-ons that you didn't agree to.

**Which of these is most likely a hidden fee?**

Description | Amount
-|-:
Car price | $\$14{,}000.00$
FSH  | $\$75.00$
Registration fee | $\$180.00$
Title transfer fee | $\$85.00$
Sales tax ($4\%$) | $\$560.00$
**Total** | **$\$14{,}900.00$**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: FSH
title transfer fee
sales tax

assessment_items.question_content:
Some dealerships employ tactics like bait and switch or add-on pressures aim to maximize their profits.

**What is a likely result of falling for a bait and switch tactic?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You spend more time at the dealership than planned.
You end up paying a lot more for the car over time.
You commit to a vehicle that exceeds your original budget.
You feel pressured into purchasing unnecessary add-ons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A dealership offered you financing on your new car and the monthly payment is much lower than you initially calculated. They did this by extending your loan term.

**What's a downside to choosing a car loan with the lower monthly payments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You get tricked into a different car at the last minute.
Extra charges pop up after you've signed for the low payments.
You end up paying a lot more in interest over time.
You're talked into buying extras you don't need, making the loan bigger.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are looking over the paperwork for your new car and notice several dealer add-ons on the invoice.

**What's a problem with agreeing to unnecessary add-ons when financing a car?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You can only choose add-ons before the purchase is finalized.

The loan amount stays the same, but your interest
rate increases.
You will not be able to trade the car in without the add-ons.
Your overall loan amount increases because of the add-ons.
